title: How Much Augmentation Is Too Much?
concept: While data augmentation makes models robust, adding too many aggressive transformations can distort images beyond recognition or change their class. Finding the right balance is key to successful training.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main difference between translation and shearing?

options.text: Translation shifts the image upright; shearing slants the image along an axis
Translation changes colors; shearing only moves pixel positions
Translation is applied to validation data; shearing to training data

exercises.explain: Right! Translation slides upright; shearing slants.

cards.title: A Blurry, Tilted Mess
cards.body: If you stack random flips, rotations, crops, shifts, and shears all at once with maximum settings, you might turn a clear photo of a dog into a tiny, tilted, half-cutoff smudge. If a human can no longer recognize the object, the model definitely won't learn from it.

cards.title: Augmentation Limits
cards.body: Extreme augmentation occurs when transformations are so strong that they distort the image beyond recognition, change its semantic class, or make it look completely unrealistic. The goal is to match the variety the model will actually see at deployment.

cards.title: Adding spice to food
cards.body: Adding a pinch of pepper makes a dish taste better. Dumping the entire jar of pepper on a plate makes it completely inedible. Augmentation is the spice of machine learning: a little bit adds great flavor and robustness, but too much ruins the whole meal.

cards.title: Flipping a 6
cards.body: Rotating a handwritten '6' by 180 degrees turns it into a '9'. If the label remains '6', you are teaching the model that a 9 is a 6! This is a class leakage error, where geometric changes change the ground truth meaning.

cards.title: More transforms aren't always better
cards.body: It's tempting to think that applying every single transformation you know at max intensity will make the model invincible. In reality, over-distorted training data makes learning too difficult, causing the model underperform on clean, normal images.

cards.title: Balanced vs. Over-Augmented
cards.body: Finding the sweet spot in transformation intensity:

left.text:
Slight shifts, tilts, and crops
Images remain realistic
Model learns robust patterns

right.text:
Severe distortion and extreme crops
Images look like abstract noise
Model fails to converge or learn

exercises.prompt: What is 'class leakage' in geometric data augmentation?

options.text: When a transformation changes the actual meaning or class of the image (like rotating a '6' into a '9')
When the training loop runs out of memory
When an image is converted from RGB to grayscale
When validation data leaks into the training pipeline

exercises.explain: Correct! Class leakage occurs when a transformation alters the class identity of the image content.

exercises.prompt: True or false: You should increase augmentation strength until the training images look completely unrealistic to challenge the model.
exercises.explain: False. Training images should stay within realistic bounds; highly unrealistic inputs make learning ineffective.

exercises.sentence: If augmentation is too aggressive, it can change the ___ of the image, leading to label confusion.
exercises.explain: Aggressive changes can alter the meaning (like turning a letter 'M' into a 'W' by flipping it vertically).

exercises.prompt: Which of these is the best guideline for deciding if an augmentation setting is too strong?

options.text: If a human expert can no longer recognize the object or determine its class
If the training epoch takes longer than five minutes to complete
If the image has more than three color channels

exercises.explain: Correct! Human recognizability is the golden baseline for safe augmentation.

exercises.prompt: You are training a digit classifier (0-9). What is the safest way to configure rotations?

options.text: Limit random rotations to a small range (e.g. ±15 degrees) so numbers tilt slightly but never flip upside down
Use full 360-degree rotation so the model learns digits from any orientation
Disable rotations completely and only use horizontal flips
Rotations are always unsafe and should never be used

exercises.explain: Exactly! Small tilts are safe; massive rotations turn digits like 6 into 9 or create invalid characters. Next, let's explore photometric augmentation — editing color and light!
